intitule: Exercise 1: Identify the correct comparative form (Resource - Easy to Medium)

enonce.texte:
Instruction: For each sentence, choose the correct degree of comparison (positive, comparative, or superlative) to complete the sentence logically.

Consigne : Pour chaque phrase, choisis le degré de comparaison correct (positif, comparatif ou superlatif) pour compléter la phrase logiquement.

questions.texte: The climate in Douala is _______ (hot) than in Dschang.
questions.reponse: hotter

questions.texte: Mount Cameroon is the _______ (high) mountain in West Africa.
questions.reponse: highest

questions.texte: This maths exercise is not _______ (difficult) as the one we did yesterday.
questions.reponse: as difficult

questions.texte: Her results are getting _______ (bad) and _______ (bad).
questions.reponse: worse and worse

questions.texte: Of all the candidates, Mrs. Mbarga has the _______ (little) experience in governance.
questions.reponse: least

enonce.texte:
Instruction: Transform the following sentences using the comparative or superlative form, as indicated in brackets.

Consigne : Transforme les phrases suivantes en utilisant la forme comparative ou superlative, comme indiqué entre parenthèses.

questions.texte: The N3 road is narrow. The road to Kribi is narrow. (Comparative: less...)
questions.reponse: The N3 road is less narrow than the road to Kribi. OR The road to Kribi is narrower than the N3 road.

questions.texte: Very few students in this school are as intelligent as Amina. (Superlative: the most...)
questions.reponse: Amina is the most intelligent student in this school.

questions.texte: Flying is fast. Taking the bus is slow. (Comparative: not as...as)
questions.reponse: Taking the bus is not as fast as flying.

questions.texte: No other river in Cameroon is longer than the Sanaga. (Superlative: the longest)
questions.reponse: The Sanaga is the longest river in Cameroon.

questions.texte: As we go higher up Mount Cameroon, the air becomes cold. (Double comparative: the higher..., the colder...)
questions.reponse: The higher we go up Mount Cameroon, the colder the air becomes.

intitule: Exercise 3: Solve the problem (Competence - Synthesis and Evaluation)

enonce.texte:
Context: You are the president of the 'Eco-Schools Club' at your lycée in Yaoundé. You have to choose between three projects for the end of the year. Analyze the data and answer the questions using the correct comparative and superlative structures.

  • Project A (Tree Planting): Cost: 50,000 FCFA | Impact on environment: Very High | Durability: 10 years | Number of students involved: 50
  • Project B (Plastic Recycling Workshop): Cost: 30,000 FCFA | Impact on environment: Medium | Durability: 1 year | Number of students involved: 100
  • Project C (Clean-up Campaign): Cost: 20,000 FCFA | Impact on environment: Low | Durability: 3 months | Number of students involved: 200

Contexte : Vous êtes le président du club 'Eco-Schools' de votre lycée à Yaoundé. Vous devez choisir entre trois projets pour la fin de l'année. Analysez les données et répondez aux questions en utilisant les structures comparatives et superlatives correctes.

questions.texte: Compare the cost of the three projects. Which project is the least expensive?
questions.reponse: Project C (20,000 FCFA) is the least expensive. / Project A (50,000 FCFA) is more expensive than Project B and Project C.

questions.texte: Analyze the 'impact on the environment'. Which project has a higher impact than Project B but a lower impact than Project A? What does that make Project A?
questions.reponse: None of the projects has an impact higher than B and lower than A. Project A has the highest impact. Project B's impact is lower than A's but higher than C's.

questions.texte: Your main goal is long-term environmental protection (durability). Justify your choice of project using a superlative.
questions.reponse: I choose Project A because it has the longest durability (10 years), which is better for long-term environmental protection.

questions.texte: The club president says: 'The more students are involved, the better the awareness.' According to this logic, which project is the best and why?
questions.reponse: According to the logic 'The more students involved, the better the awareness,' Project C is the best because it involves the highest number of students (200).

questions.texte: Write one sentence comparing Project B and Project C in terms of cost and number of students involved. Use 'not as...as' and 'fewer...than'.
questions.reponse: Project B is not as cheap as Project C, but it involves fewer students than Project C.

intitule: Exercise 4: Complete the dialogue (Competence - Application in context)

enonce.texte:
Instruction: Complete the conversation between two students, Paul and Grace, using the correct form of the adjectives in brackets (comparative or superlative).

Consigne : Complète la conversation entre deux élèves, Paul et Grace, en utilisant la forme correcte des adjectifs entre parenthèses (comparatif ou superlatif).

questions.texte: **Paul:** "I think History is _______ (interesting) than Geography."
questions.reponse: more interesting

questions.texte: **Grace:** "Maybe, but Geography is _______ (useful) subject for understanding climate change."
questions.reponse: the most useful

questions.texte: **Paul:** "That's true. But History teaches us about the past. For example, the construction of the Lagdo dam was one of _______ (big) projects of the 1980s."
questions.reponse: the biggest

questions.texte: **Grace:** "Yes, but today, environmental problems are even _______ (pressing) than historical events. We need solutions now."
questions.reponse: more pressing

questions.texte: **Paul:** "You're right. The _______ (soon) we act, the _______ (good) it will be for our planet."
questions.reponse: sooner, better
